expectations.text: Easing uses entrance (power2.out) or transition (power2.inOut), NOT spring or elastic
expectations.evidence: All easings found: power2.out (entrance), power2.inOut (transition on hero image), power2.in (nav exit), none (parallax scrub). Zero instances of elastic, back.out, or spring easings.

expectations.text: Hero section has parallax effect (yPercent with scrub:true) on desktop only
expectations.evidence: Lines 309-322: gsap.to('.hero-image', { yPercent: -12 }) with scrollTrigger { scrub: 1.5 }, guarded by `if (!isTouch)` check.

expectations.text: Ritual cards have staggered scroll reveal

expectations.text: autoAlpha used instead of bare opacity

expectations.text: No layout-triggering properties animated (no width, height, top, left, margin)
expectations.evidence: Animated GSAP properties are exclusively: y, yPercent, autoAlpha, scale. No layout-triggering properties in any gsap call.

expectations.text: GSAP and ScrollTrigger scripts are added
expectations.evidence: GSAP 3.12.5 and ScrollTrigger loaded via CDN. gsap.registerPlugin(ScrollTrigger) called.

expectations.text: Phase A identifies issues: no scroll animations (CRITICAL), no reduced-motion guard (CRITICAL)
expectations.evidence: Issues listed as 'High' and 'Medium' severity, not 'CRITICAL'. No formal Phase A structure.

expectations.text: Phase B adds animations from motion-dev pattern library
expectations.evidence: Animations added but not from any pattern library. No reference to motion-dev, catalog.yaml, or pattern names.

expectations.text: Hero heading gets kinetic typography (SplitText)
expectations.evidence: No SplitText plugin loaded or used. Hero uses manual .line/.line-inner wrappers instead.

expectations.text: Project items get stagger scroll reveal
expectations.evidence: Per-item ScrollTrigger timelines with staggered title/meta/year reveals.

expectations.text: About section gets scroll-triggered entrance
expectations.evidence: Three separate ScrollTrigger animations for .about-label, .about-text, and .skills-list li.

expectations.text: gsap.matchMedia wraps all animation code
expectations.evidence: gsap.matchMedia is NOT used. Instead, window.matchMedia with if/else block. Not the GSAP-integrated API.

expectations.text: Reduced-motion fallback uses clearProps or sets elements to visible state
expectations.evidence: No clearProps or explicit visible-state fallback. Elements using autoAlpha may remain invisible if animations are skipped.

expectations.text: Summary report follows the specified format with APPLIED/SKIPPED/N-A status per pattern
expectations.evidence: Freeform narrative format. No APPLIED/SKIPPED/N-A status labels found.

expectations.text: No CSS transition conflicts with newly added GSAP animations
expectations.evidence: Multiple CSS transitions on elements that also receive GSAP animations. E.g., .project-title has CSS transition on transform but GSAP also animates transforms.

notes: Baseline used window.matchMedia instead of gsap.matchMedia, left CSS transition conflicts, no structured report format.
Hero timeline 4+ chained calls is the only persistent failure (eval 1 with-skill). Portfolio has minimal hero (h1 + subtitle only).
gsap.matchMedia is the most discriminating assertion — 4/4 with-skill pass, 0/4 baseline pass.
Token comments and clearProps fallback are 100% skill-exclusive features.
Baseline scored higher on eval 2 (62%) because the wellness prompt is more directive.
Token overhead ~2.4x, time overhead ~2x — acceptable for the +59% quality delta.
Iteration 1 → 2: delta grew from +47% to +59% due to tighter assertions.
